g.text: 802 Bankview Dr, Drumheller, Alberta | File VAL251026
Property
Analysis
LOCATION
The subject property is located at 802 Bankview Drive in the Bankview neighbourhood of Drumheller, Alberta, on
the south side of the Red Deer River valley just across Highway 9 from the main townsite. The area is a quiet
residential community of single-family homes and low-rise apartments with convenient highway access to
Drumheller’s commercial core, health services, and major attractions in the Canadian Badlands.
Access
• Highway 9 / South Railway Avenue – primary east–west highway and commercial corridor
• Highway 56 – north–south connector to regional centres
• Highway 575 / South Dinosaur Trail – east–west route along the valley
Public Transportation
Public transportation in Drumheller is limited, with no fixed-route local transit system. Residents typically rely on
private vehicles, taxis, or limited regional shuttle/coach services. The area is therefore primarily auto-oriented for
daily travel.
Walk/Bike/Transit Scores
Bankview is a car-dependent neighbourhood. Estimated Walk Score is around 28, Bike Score in the mid-40s, and
Transit Score approximately 10, reflecting limited transit and reliance on vehicle travel.
Local Area
Bankview is a stable, low-density residential neighbourhood featuring detached homes and low-rise apartments.
The property benefits from close driving access to Drumheller’s Highway 9 commercial corridor, where groceries,
restaurants, retail, and essential services are located. Nearby amenities include the Drumheller Health Centre, local
parks, valley pathways, and major attractions such as the Royal Tyrrell Museum and the surrounding Badlands. The
location offers small-town livability with convenient access to recreation and services.
Demographics
Drumheller has an estimated population of roughly 7,900 residents, with a median age in the mid-40s and
household sizes averaging around 2.2 people. Average household income as of 2022 was $98,845 per year The
community features a mix of families, seniors, and working adults, with employment primarily in retail, tourism,
healthcare, trades, and public administration. Median household incomes fall near the provincial mid-range, and
the area maintains an affordable cost of living relative to larger Alberta centres.
Nearby Schools
• Greentree Elementary School (K–6, public)
• Drumheller Valley Secondary School (Grades 7–12, public)
• St. Anthony’s School (K–12, Catholic)
• Drumheller Outreach School (Grades 10–12, alternative program)
